title: cistus umbellatus subvar. villosissimus
intro: Cistus umbellatus subvar. villosissimus, also known as the hairy or woolly rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rockrose family, Cistaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of skin conditions such as wounds, eczema, and dermatitis due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. The hairy rockrose can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, making it a popular choice in traditional medicine and herbalism.
